claim: Opal (Google Labs) enables no-code AI workflow building by allowing drag-and-drop node composition combined with natural language descriptions of workflow intent
actionable: Use Opal for rapid prototyping of LLM workflows when you want to avoid coding but need complex agent logic
source_title: Opal - Google Labs Killer NEW App

claim: Opal provides built-in access to Gemini models, image generation (Imagen 4), audio generation (Lyria), web search, and video understanding within workflows
actionable: Leverage Opal's built-in integrations with Google's AI services for quick multimodal workflow development
source_title: Opal - Google Labs Killer NEW App

claim: User inputs can be passed through multiple workflow steps and customized based on context - editing prompts in Opal automatically wires data dependencies
actionable: When building workflows in Opal, use explicit user inputs and wire them through multiple steps for dynamic customization
source_title: Opal - Google Labs Killer NEW App

claim: Opal workflows can be remixed from gallery examples, providing starting templates that users can customize rather than building from scratch
actionable: Start with Opal gallery examples and customize them incrementally rather than building complex workflows from blank canvas
source_title: Opal - Google Labs Killer NEW App

claim: Opal workflows can be used as proof-of-concepts that can later be translated to code, bridging no-code tools and software development workflows
actionable: Use Opal for validation and prototyping, then codify successful workflows using the prompts and logic patterns from Opal
source_title: Opal - Google Labs Killer NEW App
